_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use conversation skills for gathering information, contributing to discussions, acknowledging other opinions, and linking our responses to what others have said.

_source.w: We are learning to use conversation skills for gathering information, contributing to discussions, acknowledging other opinions, and linking our responses to what others have said.
y: Effective discussion skills help us learn collaboratively and build on each other's thinking.
Acknowledging different opinions is a sign of mature, respectful communication.
Linking our responses to others shows we are actively listening and thinking.
eg: We ask focused questions to gather information for a class project.
We acknowledge a classmate's opinion before sharing our own — 'That's an interesting point. I also think…'.
We link our response to what the previous speaker said — 'Building on what Maya said…'.
We practise summarising someone else's viewpoint before agreeing or disagreeing.
We contribute evidence-based ideas during a group discussion about a text.
We reflect after a discussion on how listening to others changed our thinking.
